exercises.title: Identify composition
exercises.scenario: Recognize the has-a relationship in code.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank: a Car class that CONTAINS an Engine object is using ______.

blanks.hint: The type of the contained class

hints: Composition: field whose type is another class
Car HAS an Engine → Engine is the field type

exercises.title: Method chain through composition
exercises.scenario: Trace method calls through composed objects.
hints: u.getUsername() returns 'aisha_k'
u.getCity() returns the City object 'hyd'
hyd.getName() returns 'Hyderabad'
Chain: u.getCity().getName()

exercises.title: Simple composition
exercises.scenario: Create two classes where one contains the other.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Publisher` class with private String name and a `Book` class with private String title and a Publisher field. Constructor + getters + toString.

In main, create publisher and book, then print the book (which shows publisher info).

Expected output:
Book[Java Basics by O'Reilly]
exercises.starter_code:
// Create Publisher and Book classes
// Book has-a Publisher (composition)
hints: Publisher is a simple class with name
Book has a Publisher field (not String)
toString calls publisher.getName() via the composed object

exercises.title: Person with Address
exercises.scenario: Classic composition example with full OOP treatment.
exercises.instructions:
Create an immutable `Address` (final fields, getters, toString). Create a `Person` with name and Address field.

Print in format: 'Aisha lives at 123 MG Road, Hyderabad'

Expected output:
Aisha lives at 123 MG Road, Hyderabad
exercises.starter_code: // Create Address (immutable) and Person with composed Address

test_cases.expected_stdout: Aisha lives at 123 MG Road, Hyderabad

hints: Address has final fields (immutable)
Person constructor takes Address (not individual street/city)
p.getAddress() calls Address.toString() implicitly in string concat

exercises.title: Team with array of Members
exercises.scenario: Class that contains an array of another class.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Member` class (name, role). Create a `Team` class with name and Member[5] members field.
- `addMember(Member m)` adds to array (up to 5)
- `printRoster()` prints all members

In main:
- Create team 'Engineering'
- Add 3 members
- Print roster

Expected output:
Engineering Team:
Aisha — Backend
Raj — Frontend
Priya — DevOps
exercises.starter_code:
// Create Member and Team classes
// Team has-many Members (array)

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Engineering Team:
Aisha — Backend
Raj — Frontend
Priya — DevOps

hints: Member array in Team: Member[] members = new Member[5]
int count tracks how many members are added
addMember: members[count++] = m
printRoster: loop 0 to count-1
Access member fields through getters

exercises.title: Fix the null composition bug
exercises.scenario: NPE when composed field is null.
exercises.instructions:
This code crashes. Fix it by checking for null before using the address.

Expected output:
Aisha: 123 MG Road, Hyderabad
Raj: Address not set

bugs.issue: address.toString() crashes if address is null — NullPointerException
bugs.fix: Check if address == null first; return default message if so

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Aisha: 123 MG Road, Hyderabad
Raj: Address not set

hints: address can be null when created with 1-arg constructor
Calling any method on null → NullPointerException
Guard: if (address == null) return 'Address not set'
Defensive coding for optional composed fields

exercises.title: Defensive copy in getter
exercises.scenario: Protect internal array from external modification.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Playlist` class with private Song[] tracks (max 10) and int count. Songs have a name.

- `addTrack(Song s)` adds to array
- `getTracks()` returns a DEFENSIVE COPY using Arrays.copyOf(tracks, count)

In main:
- Create playlist, add 3 songs
- Call getTracks(), modify the returned array
- Verify original playlist still has 3 tracks (not modified)

Expected output:
Playlist tracks: 3
After modifying returned copy: still 3

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Playlist tracks: 3
After modifying returned copy: still 3

hints: Arrays.copyOf(tracks, count) creates a new array with only filled slots
Modifying the copy doesn't affect the original 'tracks' array
The count stays 3 because the original array is untouched

exercises.title: Order with LineItems
exercises.scenario: Classic e-commerce composition — Order has LineItems.
exercises.instructions:
Create:
- `LineItem` class: product name (String), quantity (int), unitPrice (double). lineTotal() method.
- `Order` class: orderId (int, auto-assigned from static), LineItem[] (max 10), `addItem(LineItem)`, `orderTotal()` returns sum of all lineTotals.

In main:
- Create order, add 3 items, print total

Expected output:
Order #1 total: ₹3598.0
exercises.starter_code:
// Create LineItem and Order classes
// Order has-many LineItems
hints: LineItem.lineTotal = quantity × unitPrice
Order.orderTotal sums all item lineTotals
Order auto-id via static nextId

exercises.title: Module 4 synthesis: full class with all patterns
exercises.scenario: Write a class using all 6 Module 4 topics.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Vehicle` class using ALL of these:
- Static counter for auto-id (4.5)
- Static final constant: MIN_YEAR = 1900 (4.5)
- Private final id, private make, model, year (4.3)
- Constructor validates year >= MIN_YEAR (4.2)
- Getter for all fields (4.3)
- Composed Engine object (4.6): Engine has horsepower (int) and fuelType (String)
- toString showing all info (4.4)
- equals by id (4.4)

In main, create 2 vehicles and print them.

Expected output:
Vehicle[#1 BMW 3-Series 2022, Engine(300hp, Petrol)]
Vehicle[#2 Tesla Model 3 2023, Engine(450hp, Electric)]

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Vehicle[#1 BMW 3-Series 2022, Engine(300hp, Petrol)]
Vehicle[#2 Tesla Model 3 2023, Engine(450hp, Electric)]

hints: All 6 patterns at once: static fields, constants, constructors, encapsulation, Object methods, composition
Vehicle has-a Engine → Engine is a field
toString shows engine via engine.toString() — implicit in string concat

exercises.title: Hospital appointment system
exercises.scenario: Multi-class composition modeling a real domain.
exercises.instructions:
Create:
- `Doctor` class: name (String), specialty (String). Getters. toString.
- `Patient` class: name (String), age (int). Getters. toString.
- `Appointment` class: Doctor, Patient, date (String). Method `describe()` returns full appointment info.

In main, create 2 appointments and print descriptions.

Expected output:
Appointment: Dr. Priya (Cardiology) with Raj (35) on 2024-02-15
Appointment: Dr. Aisha (Dermatology) with Vikram (28) on 2024-02-16
exercises.starter_code:
// Create Doctor, Patient, Appointment classes
// Appointment has-a Doctor and a Patient

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Appointment: Dr. Priya (Cardiology) with Raj (35) on 2024-02-15
Appointment: Dr. Aisha (Dermatology) with Vikram (28) on 2024-02-16

hints: Appointment has-a Doctor AND a Patient
describe() uses doctor.toString() and patient.toString() implicitly
Three independent classes compose into one system

exercises.title: Find most expensive item in composed structure
exercises.scenario: Iterate composed objects to find a value.
exercises.instructions:
Using a Cart that has CartItem[] (each CartItem has product name and price):
- Create a cart with 5 items at different prices
- Write method `mostExpensiveItem()` returning the CartItem with highest price

Expected output:
Most expensive: Gaming Monitor at ₹24999.0
exercises.starter_code:
// Create CartItem and Cart classes
// Cart.mostExpensiveItem() iterates composed CartItems
hints: mostExpensiveItem returns a CartItem object (not just a double!)
Start max = items[0]
Compare items[i].getPrice() > max.getPrice()
Return the CartItem itself — the caller can get any info from it

exercises.title: Module 4 recap: class with everything
exercises.scenario: Synthesis exercise — write a well-designed class from scratch.
exercises.instructions:
Design a `Product` class FROM SCRATCH with ALL Module 4 patterns:

- static int nextId counter
- static final double TAX_RATE = 0.18
- private final id, private name, price, category
- Constructor: validates name not empty, price > 0
- Getters
- `priceWithTax()` computed method: price * (1 + TAX_RATE)
- toString: 'Product[#id name — ₹price (₹tax incl.)]'
- equals by id
- hashCode by id

In main, create 2 products, print including tax.

Expected output:
Product[#1 Laptop — ₹50000.0 (₹59000.0 incl.)]
Product[#2 Phone — ₹30000.0 (₹35400.0 incl.)]
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.Objects;

// Design Product class with ALL Module 4 patterns from memory

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Product[#1 Laptop — ₹50000.0 (₹59000.0 incl.)]
Product[#2 Phone — ₹30000.0 (₹35400.0 incl.)]

hints: All patterns from memory — no peeking!
priceWithTax = price * 1.18

exercises.title: Library with Books and Members
exercises.scenario: Full library system with 3 classes, composition, and operations.
exercises.instructions:
Build a mini library system:

1. `Book` class: title, author, isbn. isAvailable() (boolean). checkOut() and returnBook() change availability.

2. `Member` class: name, id (auto-assigned). toString.

3. `Library` class: name, Book[] (max 20), Member[] (max 50). addBook(), addMember(), checkOut(memberId, isbn) method, getAvailableCount().

In main:
- Add 3 books, 2 members
- Check out one book
- Print available count

Expected output:
Available books: 2
exercises.starter_code:
// Build Book, Member, Library classes
// Library orchestrates composition
hints: Three classes, each with own responsibilities
Library orchestrates the composition — it contains Books and Members
checkOut finds book by isbn using .equals(), marks unavailable
getAvailableCount loops and checks isAvailable()

exercises.title: School with Departments and Courses
exercises.scenario: Three levels of composition — Department contains Courses.
exercises.instructions:
Three-level composition:
- `Course` class: code (String), title, credits (int)
- `Department` class: name, Course[] (max 20), `addCourse()`, `totalCredits()` sums all course credits
- `School` class: name, Department[] (max 10), `addDepartment()`, `findByCode(String code)` searches all departments' courses and returns the Course or null

In main:
- Create a school with 2 departments, each with 2-3 courses
- Print total credits in one department
- Find a course by code

Expected output:
Engineering credits: 12
Found: CS202 Data Structures (4 credits)

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Engineering credits: 12
Found: CS202 Data Structures (4 credits)

hints: Three levels: School → Department → Course
totalCredits loops through Department's courses
school.findByCode: search each department, return first match
3 + 4 + 5 = 12 engineering credits

exercises.title: Module 4 capstone: E-commerce system
exercises.scenario: Full OOP design — every pattern from every topic.
exercises.instructions:
Build a mini e-commerce system WITH EVERY MODULE 4 PATTERN:

1. `Category` (value class): name, description. Immutable.
2. `Product` (entity): static auto-id, static final MAX_PRICE, private fields, validating constructor, getters, setters with validation, computed `discountedPrice(double percent)`, toString, equals by id, hashCode.
3. `CartItem`: Product + quantity. lineTotal() method.
4. `ShoppingCart`: CartItem[] (max 20), addItem(Product, qty), cartTotal(), `countItems()`, toString.

In main: add 3 products to cart, print cart total.

Expected output:
Cart total: ₹5097.0
hints: USB Hub 499×2 = 998, Keyboard 1999×1 = 1999, Mouse 599×1 = 599 → total 3596
Category → Product (composition: has-a Category)
CartItem → Product (composition: has-a Product)
ShoppingCart → CartItem[] (composition: has-many CartItems)
Three levels of composition
